Interval Comparison Lab
Compare two close interval sounds, then load the pair into practice.
Interval Inversion Lab
Choose an interval to see its inversion and how it reduces inside the octave.
Direction and Texture Lab
Choose a direction lens to connect the same interval names to melodic and harmonic listening.
Context and Scale Degree Lab
Choose a musical context to connect interval names to scale degrees, solfege, chord color, and real usage.
Melody Pattern Lab
Choose a short melodic shape to hear intervals inside a real phrase instead of one isolated jump.
Interval Dictation Lab
Choose a short pattern to practice holding a phrase in memory and naming the interval moves.
Interval Checkpoint Lab
Choose a checkpoint to load a focused review set and check whether the interval group is ready.
Interval Mastery Coach
Choose a coach view to turn interval results into a short practice plan.
Interval Session Builder
Choose a session goal and length to build a complete interval practice plan.
Interval Exam Builder
Choose an exam to build a scored readiness check from the interval labs.
Interval Completion Dashboard
Review the full interval page and build a final plan.
Interval Assignment Pack
Build a clean homework or lesson assignment from the current interval results.
Interval Teacher Review Board
Review interval groups by status and choose the next lesson move.

How To Use

Improv Trainer

Read the chord symbol, play the chord tones from memory, and move forward only when the answer is correct. Use the timeline, metronome, count off, backing pulse, progression library, guide-tone line practice, call and response phrase echo work, motif variation, approach-tone resolution, Improv Challenge, and the Improv Path to connect chord work to real musical flow.

How To Use
Look at the chord symbol and play the required tones from memory. Use Voicing Target for full chords, shells, guide tones, or rootless 9ths. Target Note Drill trains single landing notes. Approach Tone Drill trains one or two notes resolving into the selected target role. Phrase Builder asks for a short melodic phrase from the selected Solo Source and checks that the final note lands on the target role. Guide Tone Line trains the 3rd and 7th movement through the selected progression. Call and Response plays a short model phrase for you to echo by ear. Choose Progression Timeline, set the count off beside the metronome, choose a Backing sound, press Start, and follow the highlighted chord in time. Use Improv Challenge as the final mixed review across chords, targets, guide tones, phrases, and approaches. Use Practice Plan and the Step Timer when you want a timed routine. Use Coach Pick for one recommended next action. Use Session Recap when you want a clean summary of the current practice session. Use Improv Path when you want a guided order for the tools.
Quick Focus
Choose a preset to load a focused setup.
One tap setups for common improv practice. Timeline presets still use Start.
Mood Practice Bridge
Choose a mood to turn theory into a playable setup.
This bridges the Theory mood lens into real improv practice.
Choose a mood, then load it into Improv.
Educational bridge only. It loads existing Improv controls without changing the locked pages.
Mood Loop Lab
Load a mood, then start a simple loop for practice.
Use this as a small backing bed while you practice the loaded mood.
Practice Plan
Choose a plan and load the first step.
Step Timer 00:00
Start the timer after loading the current step.
A simple routine built from Quick Focus presets. Use Load Step, practice, then move to the next step.
